vulnerabilities.title: Command Injection via system() with String Concatenation
vulnerabilities.description: `system()` is called with a dynamically constructed command string (CWE-78). If any portion of the string is derived from user input, shell metacharacters (`; | && ||`) can be injected to run arbitrary commands. Use `execve()` with argument arrays instead of `system()`.

vulnerabilities.title: Use After delete on Raw Pointer
vulnerabilities.description: Pointer `data` is deleted at line 13 and then accessed at line 15 (CWE-416). Accessing freed memory causes undefined behaviour and can be exploited for arbitrary code execution. Set the pointer to `nullptr` immediately after `delete`.
vulnerabilities.standards_citation: [CERT MEM30-C] CVSS: 8.1 (HIGH) | A08:2021-Software and Data Integrity Failures

vulnerabilities.title: Format String Injection via printf()
vulnerabilities.description: `printf()` is called with a non-literal format string (CWE-134). If the format string is attacker-controlled, format specifiers like `%n` can be used to read or write arbitrary memory. Always use `printf("%s", variable)` with a hard-coded literal as the format.

vulnerabilities.title: Format String Injection via fprintf()
vulnerabilities.description: `fprintf()` is called with a non-literal format string (CWE-134). If the format string is attacker-controlled, format specifiers like `%n` can be used to read or write arbitrary memory. Always use `fprintf("%s", variable)` with a hard-coded literal as the format.

vulnerabilities.title: Integer Overflow in Array Size Calculation
vulnerabilities.description: The size expression passed to `new[]` contains an arithmetic operation on integer values (CWE-190). If the result wraps around (overflows), a smaller-than-expected buffer is allocated, leading to a heap buffer overflow when it is filled. Cast values to `size_t` and validate against maximum bounds before allocation.

vulnerabilities.title: Memory Leak via Raw new without delete
vulnerabilities.description: A raw `new` expression allocates heap memory but no corresponding `delete` was found in the same scope (CWE-401). This causes a memory leak. Prefer `std::unique_ptr<T>` or `std::shared_ptr<T>` for automatic resource management.

vulnerabilities.title: Potential Null Pointer Dereference (No Exception Guard)
vulnerabilities.description: A pointer is dereferenced without a preceding null check and the function has no try-catch handler (CWE-476). If the pointer is null, the program will throw `std::bad_alloc` or segfault. Always validate pointers before dereferencing or use references with RAII smart pointers.

vulnerabilities.title: File Created via ofstream without Explicit Permissions
vulnerabilities.description: A file is created using `std::ofstream` without explicitly setting restrictive file permissions (CWE-732). The file will inherit the process umask, which might allow other users to read sensitive data. After creation, call `chmod()` with `0600` or set `umask(0077)` before opening.

vulnerabilities.title: Fixed char Array — Prefer std::string
vulnerabilities.description: A fixed-size `char` array is declared in a C++ context (CWE-119). Manual size management is error-prone and can lead to buffer overflow if input exceeds the declared size. Replace with `std::string` or `std::array<char, N>` with bounds-checked operations.
